About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.

Zegona Communications plc

The Company has been informed that Suzi Williams purchased 2500 ordinary shares of £0.01 pence each in the Company ("
Ordinary Shares
") at a price of £16.8334 per share on 4 March 2026
.

latest.title: FiberPass Transaction Completes
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 6651V
Zegona Communications PLC
06 March 2026
6 March 2026
LEI: 213800ASI1VZL2ED4S65
Zegona Communications plc
FiberPass Transaction Completes
Zegona is pleased to announce
that on 5 March 2026, AXA's1 acquisition of a 40% stake in FiberPass2 completed
.
Vodafone Spain has
generated up front proceeds of ?0.4bn from this transaction. The
ownership of FiberPass is now 55% Telef
ó
nica, 40% AXA and 5% Vodafone Spain.
?0.2
bn of the proceeds will be used to fund the share buyback programme announced on 27 November 2025.
The remaining
?0.2
bn will be used for debt reduction and puts the business in a good position to deliver net debt of only
?3.2bn by the end of our financial year, March 2026.
This reinforces Zegona's commitment to its leverage target of 1.5X - 2X and further accelerates progress in reducing total annual interest costs3.
Eamonn O'Hare, Chairman and CEO of Zegona, commented, "The AXA investment completes our transformation of Vodafone Spain's fixed network strategy. This transaction delivers significant proceeds which help drive returns to Zegona shareholders through our share buyback programme, a step change in net debt reduction and an acceleration in reducing total annual interest costs."
For further information, please contact:
Investor enquiries:
Media enquiries:
Alfonso Enríquez
Jaime De Andres
Tilly Abraham (Sodali & Co)
info
@zegona.com
jaime.andres@vodafone.com
zegona@info.sodali.com
About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns.
?
Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.
?
About Vodafone Spain
Vodafone Spain is a national provider of fixed, mobile and TV services in Spain, serving consumer, business and public administration customers. It was acquired by Zegona in 2024.
About Telef
ó
nica
Telefónica is one of the world's leading telecommunications service providers. The company offers fixed and mobile connectivity as well as a wide range of digital services for residential and business customers. With over 350 million customers, Telefónica operates in Europe and Latin America. Telefónica is listed on the Spanish stock market, New York and Lima.
Notes
1. AXA means BNPP AM Alts,
part of the BNP Paribas Group since 1st July 2025
2. FiberPass is
Compañía Mayorista de Fibra, S.L.,
the fibre joint venture between Vodafone Spain, Telefonica and BNPP AM Alts
3. Applying current market debt yields to the reduced net debt balance demonstrates the potential to drive annual interest costs well below
?200m
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: ZEG.L runs a national fibre‑optic network. It owns, operates and leases fibre cables to telecom operators, cloud providers and other high‑bandwidth customers. The company also offers managed network services and infrastructure support.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The fibre backbone is the backbone of tomorrow’s digital economy – 5G, cloud, AI and high‑speed broadband all need fibre. ZEG.L controls a sizable portion of the UK’s fibre assets, giving it long‑term contracts and a moat against new entrants. The sector is expanding as data traffic grows, making ZEG.L a key player in a high‑demand market.
company_30s.current_state: The firm has recently finished a major fibre‑upgrade deal (FiberPass), announced a dividend, and was added to the MSCI UK Index. Governance changes and share‑holding adjustments suggest a re‑alignment of strategy. These moves signal a company that is stabilising after a sharp decline and preparing for renewed growth.

rns_narrative: Corporate governance and share‑holding changes indicate a shift in leadership and ownership structure.
The completion of the FiberPass transaction shows operational progress and a new revenue stream.
Being added to the MSCI UK Index boosts liquidity and brings institutional investors into the picture.
The dividend announcement and financial reporting demonstrate the company’s intent to return value to shareholders.

crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bulls point to the dividend, fibre expansion and index inclusion as catalysts that could lift the stock.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears worry about rising debt, intense competition in the fibre market and the risk that the dividend may not be sustainable.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from silence to a mild, hopeful tone as the index move and dividend spark new interest.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat – the market is largely unaware of ZEG.L, leaving room for a surprise rebound.

near_term_catalysts: A new fibre‑contract announcement or expansion into a new region.
The ex‑dividend date – a volume spike could act as a buying catalyst.
Positive earnings guidance or an upgrade in debt metrics.
Regulatory approval for a major infrastructure project.
expect_next.watch_for: Key signals include a price break above the 52‑week support line, volume rising to at least three times the average, and any new contract or partnership news.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early to mid phase of a mean‑reversion cycle. The APEX score shows compression, suggesting a pullback is likely before a rebound.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: With a deep crash, the historical green‑signal average return is about 39 %. If the price recovers halfway to its 52‑week range, that’s roughly a 50 % upside. With a 10 % risk (stop 10 % below entry) the risk‑reward ratio is close to 4:1 – a highly asymmetric play.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risks are that debt levels could push the company into a refinancing crisis, competition could erode margins, or the dividend may not be sustainable. In the worst case, the stock could stay flat or decline further, leaving a 10 % loss.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Risk 10 % → Potential reward 39 % (or 50 % if the price hits halfway). Ratio roughly 4:1, making the upside far outweigh the downside.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a pullback to the 52‑week support level. Size the position so that a 10 % move against you would not exceed 5 % of the portfolio. Place a stop 10 % below entry and trail it as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until volume spikes above three times the average and the price clears the 52‑week support. A clear break and sustained volume confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
playbook.if_passing: No genuine terminal risk (delisting or bankruptcy) is evident, so the setup remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: ZEG.L’s deep crash, MSCI inclusion, and fibre‑upgrade momentum create a high‑asymmetry upside if the stock recovers halfway to its 52‑week range.
